title: Tamil Tomato Rice

ingredients.name: cooked rice (cooled)
visual_checks: day-old or freshly cooked and spread to cool; hot sticky rice does not mix well
ingredients.notes: day-old or freshly cooked and spread to cool; hot sticky rice does not mix well

subs.name: leftover rice from the fridge
subs.notes: cold rice separates better and does not turn mushy when mixed with hot masala

ingredients.name: tomato, chopped
visual_checks: ripe and slightly sour for best flavour
ingredients.notes: ripe and slightly sour for best flavour

subs.name: canned crushed tomatoes (200 g)
subs.notes: works well and cuts the cooking time of the masala by half

subs.name: 1 tsp each of finely minced fresh ginger and garlic

steps.text: Heat oil in a wide kadai on medium flame. Splutter mustard seeds, then add urad dal and fry until golden. Add curry leaves and green chilli and fry for 30 seconds.
Add onions and fry on medium-high flame, stirring frequently, until they turn golden brown at the edges — about 6 minutes. Do not rush this step; well-browned onions are the base of the masala.

Add ginger-garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes, about 1 minute. Add the chopped tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook on medium flame until they completely break down and the oil begins to separate — about 8 minutes. The masala should look dark and jammy.
Lower the flame and add red chilli powder, coriander powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds — do not let the powders burn. The masala should be dry, not watery.
Add the cooked rice in batches, folding it through the masala with a wide spatula. Be gentle to avoid breaking the grains. The rice should be evenly coated in the reddish-orange masala.
Taste and adjust salt. Scatter coriander leaves on top. Serve with pappad and raw onion slices. Excellent in lunchboxes as the flavour deepens after an hour.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tomato → canned crushed tomatoes (200 g) (works well and cuts the cooking time of the masala by half); rice → leftover rice from the fridge (cold rice separates better and does not turn mushy when mixed with hot masala); ginger-garlic-paste → 1 tsp each of finely minced fresh ginger and garlic (fresher flavour)
